title: canthium glaucum
intro: Canthium glaucum, also known as the Blue Cinnamon, is a tropical flowering plant native to India and Southeast Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Ayurvedic medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions. The leaves and bark of the plant contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, offering health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and soothing respiratory problems. Canthium glaucum can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder for various herbal remedies, including teas, capsules, and topical applications.
